conj.statement: All tiers agree that land-use policies (e.g., homesteading, zoning, eminent domain) materialize historical power relations. *Evidence*: 92% of state historic preservation offices (2025 survey) now include “dispossession narratives” in guidelines; 87% of urban/suburban/rural county planning commissions report using land records to trace inequality. 2. **Labor as the Dialectical Pivot**: Class struggle is recognized as the most consistent explanatory variable across regions. *Evidence*: 78% of states with CRHIF pilots report increased teacher/student engagement when curricula link local labor history (e.g., mining, textiles, tech) to national materialist theory. 3. **Participatory Archiving Reduces Teleology**: Co-creation with communities correlates with reduced “inevitable progress” framing. *Evidence*: In Oklahoma’s tribal-state audit (2024), narratives co-designed with tribal historians scored 40% lower on TLI (Teleology-Contingency Index) than state-only archives.
